root_text.an2.21:1.3: Yo ca accayaṁ accayato na passati, yo ca accayaṁ desentassa yathādhammaṁ nappaṭiggaṇhāti.
root_text.an2.21:2.3: Yo ca accayaṁ accayato passati, yo ca accayaṁ desentassa yathādhammaṁ paṭiggaṇhāti.
root_text.an2.22:1.3: Duṭṭho vā dosantaro, saddho vā duggahitena.
root_text.an2.22:1.4: Ime kho, bhikkhave, dve tathāgataṁ abbhācikkhantī”ti.
root_text.an2.23:1.3: Yo ca abhāsitaṁ alapitaṁ tathāgatena bhāsitaṁ lapitaṁ tathāgatenāti dīpeti, yo ca bhāsitaṁ lapitaṁ tathāgatena abhāsitaṁ alapitaṁ tathāgatenāti dīpeti.
root_text.an2.23:1.4: Ime kho, bhikkhave, dve tathāgataṁ abbhācikkhantīti.
root_text.an2.23:2.3: Yo ca abhāsitaṁ alapitaṁ tathāgatena abhāsitaṁ alapitaṁ tathāgatenāti dīpeti, yo ca bhāsitaṁ lapitaṁ tathāgatena bhāsitaṁ lapitaṁ tathāgatenāti dīpeti.
root_text.an2.23:2.4: Ime kho, bhikkhave, dve tathāgataṁ nābbhācikkhantī”ti.
root_text.an2.24:1.3: Yo ca neyyatthaṁ suttantaṁ nītattho suttantoti dīpeti, yo ca nītatthaṁ suttantaṁ neyyattho suttantoti dīpeti.
root_text.an2.24:1.4: Ime kho, bhikkhave, dve tathāgataṁ abbhācikkhantī”ti.
root_text.an2.25:1.3: Yo ca neyyatthaṁ suttantaṁ neyyattho suttantoti dīpeti, yo ca nītatthaṁ suttantaṁ nītattho suttantoti dīpeti.
root_text.an2.25:1.4: Ime kho, bhikkhave, dve tathāgataṁ nābbhācikkhantī”ti.
root_text.an2.26:1.1: “Paṭicchannakammantassa, bhikkhave, dvinnaṁ gatīnaṁ aññatarā gati pāṭikaṅkhā—
root_text.an2.26:2.1: Appaṭicchannakammantassa, bhikkhave, dvinnaṁ gatīnaṁ aññatarā gati pāṭikaṅkhā—
root_text.an2.27:1.1: “Micchādiṭṭhikassa, bhikkhave, dvinnaṁ gatīnaṁ aññatarā gati pāṭikaṅkhā—
root_text.an2.28:1.1: “Sammādiṭṭhikassa, bhikkhave, dvinnaṁ gatīnaṁ aññatarā gati pāṭikaṅkhā—
root_text.an2.30:1.1: “Dvāhaṁ, bhikkhave, atthavase sampassamāno araññavanapatthāni pantāni senāsanāni paṭisevāmi.
root_text.an2.30:1.3: Attano ca diṭṭhadhammasukhavihāraṁ sampassamāno, pacchimañca janataṁ anukampamāno.
root_text.an2.30:1.4: Ime kho ahaṁ, bhikkhave, dve atthavase sampassamāno araññavanapatthāni pantāni senāsanāni paṭisevāmī”ti.
root_text.an2.31:1.12: Rāgupakkiliṭṭhaṁ vā, bhikkhave, cittaṁ na vimuccati, avijjupakkiliṭṭhā vā paññā na bhāvīyati.
root_text.an2.31:1.13: Iti kho, bhikkhave, rāgavirāgā cetovimutti, avijjāvirāgā paññāvimuttī”ti.

translation_text.an2.21:1.3: One who doesn’t recognize when they’ve made a mistake. And one who doesn’t properly accept the confession of someone who’s made a mistake.
translation_text.an2.21:2.1: There are two who are astute.
translation_text.an2.21:2.3: One who recognizes when they’ve made a mistake. And one who properly accepts the confession of someone who’s made a mistake.
translation_text.an2.21:2.4: These are the two who are astute.”
translation_text.an2.22:1.1: “Mendicants, these two misrepresent the Realized One.
translation_text.an2.22:1.3: One who is hateful and hides it, and one whose faith is mistaken.
translation_text.an2.22:1.4: These two misrepresent the Realized One.”
translation_text.an2.23:1.1: “Mendicants, these two misrepresent the Realized One.
translation_text.an2.23:1.3: One who explains what was not spoken by the Realized One as spoken by him. And one who explains what was spoken by the Realized One as not spoken by him.
translation_text.an2.23:1.4: These two misrepresent the Realized One.
translation_text.an2.23:2.1: These two don’t misrepresent the Realized One.
translation_text.an2.23:2.3: One who explains what was not spoken by the Realized One as not spoken by him. And one who explains what was spoken by the Realized One as spoken by him.
translation_text.an2.23:2.4: These two don’t misrepresent the Realized One.”
translation_text.an2.24:1.1: “Mendicants, these two misrepresent the Realized One.
translation_text.an2.24:1.3: One who explains a discourse in need of interpretation as a discourse whose meaning is explicit. And one who explains a discourse whose meaning is explicit as a discourse in need of interpretation.
translation_text.an2.24:1.4: These two misrepresent the Realized One.”
translation_text.an2.25:1.1: “These two don’t misrepresent the Realized One.
translation_text.an2.25:1.3: One who explains a discourse in need of interpretation as a discourse in need of interpretation. And one who explains a discourse whose meaning is explicit as a discourse whose meaning is explicit.
translation_text.an2.25:1.4: These two don’t misrepresent the Realized One.”
translation_text.an2.26:1.1: “Mendicants, one who is underhand can expect one of two destinies:
translation_text.an2.26:2.1: One who is aboveboard can expect one of two destinies:
translation_text.an2.26:2.2: as a god or a human.”
translation_text.an2.27:1.1: “Mendicants, one who has wrong view can expect one of two destinies:
translation_text.an2.28:1.1: “Mendicants, one who has right view can expect one of two destinies:
translation_text.an2.28:1.2: as a god or a human.”
translation_text.an2.29:1.1: “There are two places waiting to receive an unethical person:
translation_text.an2.29:2.1: There are two places waiting to receive an ethical person:
translation_text.an2.29:2.2: the realms of gods and humans.”
translation_text.an2.30:1.1: “Mendicants, I see two reasons to frequent remote lodgings in the wilderness and the forest.
translation_text.an2.30:1.3: Seeing happiness for oneself in this life, and having sympathy for future generations.
translation_text.an2.30:1.4: I see two reasons to frequent remote lodgings in the wilderness and the forest.”
translation_text.an2.31:1.1: “These two things play a part in realization.
translation_text.an2.31:1.4: What is the benefit of developing serenity?
translation_text.an2.31:1.6: What is the benefit of developing the mind?
translation_text.an2.31:1.8: What is the benefit of developing discernment?
translation_text.an2.31:1.10: What is the benefit of developing wisdom?
translation_text.an2.31:1.12: The mind corrupted by greed is not free; and wisdom corrupted by ignorance does not grow.
translation_text.an2.31:1.13: In this way, freedom of heart comes from the fading away of greed, while freedom by wisdom comes from the fading away of ignorance.”

variant_text.an2.29:2.2: devā vā manussā vā”ti → devo vā manusso vāti (mr)
